data.title: Bitcoin Core Developers Affirm Commitment to Decentralization and User Freedom
data.description:
According to Foresight News, the Bitcoin Core development team has issued a statement asserting their stance on transaction relays with ongoing economic demand that will eventually be included by miners. While they do not encourage the use of Bitcoin for non-financial data purposes, they emphasize the importance of maintaining a censorship-resistant and decentralized system, allowing users and miners the freedom to choose transaction content.

The team highlighted that Bitcoin Core software does not automatically update, requiring users to opt for upgrades themselves. This approach underscores the fundamental values of the Bitcoin network: freedom and decentralization. They noted that in practice, users and miners can easily bypass relay restrictions, suggesting that node software should be designed to predict which transactions will be included in blocks rather than attempting to interfere with legitimate transaction activities.

The statement concluded with a commitment to continue optimizing transaction relay strategies to ensure the long-term healthy development of Bitcoin. This involves balancing system security, block construction efficiency, and resistance to attacks.

data.title: Pro-Crypto News: UK Lifts Ban, Trump’s Efforts Towards BTC Treasury
data.description:
British crypto news recently saw positive developments after regulators upended a crypto ban that was in place since 2019. The uplifted ban could pave the way for more demand for Bitcoin and altcoins from the European market.
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/06/07/crypto-news-uk-may-push-crypto-trump-takes-btc-treasury-efforts-forward/

data.title: The Hong Kong SAR government will provide more than HK$10 billion in funding for the development of artificial intelligence
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Hong Kong Chief Executive John Lee attended the opening ceremony of the 16th Cross-Strait and Hong Kong and Macao Economic and Trade Cooperation Seminar hosted by the Chinese Manufacturers' Association of Hong Kong yesterday and delivered a speech. John Lee said that Hong Kong is actively developing the artificial intelligence industry, promoting the transformation and upgrading of traditional industries, and is committed to building an international innovation and technology center. In terms of financial support, Hong Kong has launched a special funding plan of HK$3 billion for artificial intelligence to subsidize local Hong Kong colleges, research and development institutions and enterprises to use the computing power of supercomputing centers to promote scientific research breakthroughs; and will set up a "Innovation and Technology Industry Guidance Fund" with a scale of HK$10 billion to strengthen the guidance of market funds and promote the development of strategic emerging and future industries such as AI and robots. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin Repeating 2024 Rally? Analysts Eye ‘Real Breakout’ To $120,000
data.description:
Amid the Trump-Musk online feud, Bitcoin (BTC) has hovered within the mid-and-low areas of its local price range, hitting a one-month low near the $100,000 support. However, some analysts suggest that the cryptocurrency is preparing for the “real” price jump toward a new all-time high (ATH) Related Reading: SUI Rally At Risk? Analysts Warn Of 30% Dip If This Level Doesn’t Hold Bitcoin Prepares For ‘Real Breakout’ Over the past 24 hours, Bitcoin experienced significant volatility fueled by the online feud between US President Donald Trump and Tesla and X owner Elon Musk. The flagship crypto’s price took a beating on Thursday afternoon after dropping by over 5% from the $105,000 level to the $100,000 support. Before the pullback, BTC had been attempting to reclaim its local mid-range area after its recent performance
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/bitcoin/bitcoin-repeating-2024-rally-analysts-eye-real-breakout-to-120000/

data.title: China announces plans to sell seized crypto via Hong Kong exchanges
data.description:
China has announced plans to sell seized digital assets through licensed exchanges in Hong Kong. The initiative is in collaboration with the China Beijing Equity Exchange (CBEX) to manage digital assets seized in criminal cases. In line with this, CBEX will engage third-party agencies to help sell the assets on regulated exchanges. According to reports, […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/china-to-sell-seized-crypto-via-hong-kong/

data.title: European Central Bank Nears End of Rate Cuts, Says Vujcic
data.description: According to BlockBeats, European Central Bank (ECB) Governing Council member Boris Vujcic has indicated that the ECB is approaching the conclusion of its interest rate reduction cycle. Over the past year, the ECB has implemented eight rate cuts, totaling a reduction of 200 basis points.

data.title: Nasdaq-listed liquor company Heritage officially transforms into a crypto product company and accepts BTC payments
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Heritage Distilling (CASK), a Nasdaq-listed spirits manufacturer, announced the formal establishment of a cryptocurrency financial reserve strategy and the transformation from traditional consumer packaged goods to products built on blockchain technology and cryptocurrency integration, becoming a crypto product company. It also announced that its pre-sale products will support Bitcoin purchases. (Globenewswire)

data.title: Binance Market Update (2025-06-07)
data.description:
The global cryptocurrency market cap now stands at $3.23T, down by -1.82% over the last day, according to CoinMarketCap data.

Bitcoin (BTC) has been trading between $103,514 and $105,333 over the past 24 hours. As of 09:30 AM (UTC) today, BTC is trading at $104,936, up by 1.30%.

Most major cryptocurrencies by market cap are trading higher. Market outperformers include HUMA, CFX, and FLM, up by 15%, 14%, and 13%, respectively.
